summary: The server-rendered assignment, submission, and upload forms do not include any CSRF token or equivalent anti-CSRF validation. A malicious site can cause a logged-in victim to create, modify, or delete assignments, submissions, and uploads using the victim's existing authenticated session.

evidence_snippets.code:
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('assignments.create_assignment_submit', course_id=course['course_id']) }}">
  <label for="title">Title</label>
  <input id="title" name="title" required>
  <label for="description">Description</label>
  <textarea id="description" name="description" rows="6"></textarea>
  <label for="due_at">Due At</label>
  <input id="due_at" name="due_at" placeholder="2026-03-06 23:59">
evidence_snippets.reason: The assignment-creation form changes server state but includes no CSRF token or authenticity field.

evidence_snippets.code:

evidence_snippets.reason: The assignment-deletion form is forgeable because it carries no request authenticity token.

evidence_snippets.code:
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('assignments.create_submission_submit', course_id=course['course_id'], assignment_id=assignment['assignment_id']) }}">
  <label for="content_text">Submission Text</label>
  <textarea id="content_text" name="content_text" rows="8"></textarea>
  <label for="attachment_upload_id">Attachment Upload</label>
  <select id="attachment_upload_id" name="attachment_upload_id">
    <option value="">No attachment</option>
    {% for upload in uploads %}
    <option value="{{ upload['upload_id'] }}">{{ upload['original_name'] }}</option>
    {% endfor %}
evidence_snippets.reason: The submission form performs a state-changing request without any anti-CSRF field.

evidence_snippets.code:
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('assignments.update_submission_submit', course_id=course['course_id'], assignment_id=assignment['assignment_id'], submission_id=submission['submission_id']) }}">
  <label for="content_text">Submission Text</label>
  <textarea id="content_text" name="content_text" rows="8">{{ submission['content_text'] }}</textarea>
  <label for="attachment_upload_id">Attachment Upload</label>
  <select id="attachment_upload_id" name="attachment_upload_id">
    <option value="">No attachment</option>
    {% for upload in uploads %}
    <option value="{{ upload['upload_id'] }}" {% if submission['attachment_upload_id'] == upload['upload_id'] %}selected{% endif %}>{{ upload['original_name'] }}</option>
evidence_snippets.reason: The submission-update form lacks any request authenticity token.

evidence_snippets.code:
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('assignments.create_upload_submit', course_id=course['course_id']) }}" enctype="multipart/form-data">
  <label for="file">File</label>
  <input id="file" type="file" name="file" required>
  <button type="submit">Upload</button>
evidence_snippets.reason: The upload form performs a privileged state change without any CSRF protection.

evidence_snippets.code: Download
evidence_snippets.reason: The upload-deletion form is a state-changing POST with no CSRF token.
The assignment creation handler accepts cookie-authenticated browser POST requests without verifying any CSRF token.
The assignment update handler performs state changes using only cookie-based authentication.
The assignment deletion handler processes browser POST requests without request authenticity validation.
The submission create and update handlers accept authenticated browser POST requests without any anti-CSRF mechanism.
The upload creation handler accepts browser POST requests without CSRF validation.
The upload deletion handler is a state-changing POST that relies solely on the browser's authenticated cookies.

root_cause: Server-rendered assignment, submission, and upload workflows rely on cookie-based authentication but do not require a request authenticity token or equivalent CSRF defense for state-changing form submissions.
repair_plan: Introduce server-side CSRF protection for all browser assignment, submission, and upload workflows by generating a per-session token, embedding it in every state-changing form, and rejecting requests whose token is missing or invalid. Apply the same protection consistently to create, update, and delete actions across assignments, submissions, and uploads.
